content:
Your Name Tells a Lot

Your name tells a lot,
Credentials do not.
Frames upon the wall may shine,
Yet truth refuses borrowed light.

You may gather countless degrees,
Certificates carried on every breeze.
Scopus papers, one by one,
Books authored till the shelves are done.

Edited volumes, patents filed,
Academic records neatly compiled.
Titles rise before your name,
But wisdom cannot be claimed the same.

For knowledge is not ink on a page,
Nor rank, nor office, nor learned stage.
It lives in questions honestly asked,
In truths pursued, in egos unmasked.

I have seen scholars in lofty chairs,
Surrounded by applause and affairs.
Yet when the simplest question came,
Silence whispered louder than fame.

A humble mind with a curious soul
Often understands the greater whole.
One clear thought, sincerely earned,
Outshines a thousand lessons learned.

Let your character write your story,
Not borrowed applause or passing glory.
For when the titles fade away,
Only knowledge chooses to stay.

Your name tells a lot.
Credentials do not.
If you truly possess knowledge,
Your name alone is enough.
comment: Credentials open doors and signal effort, but they don't replace judgment. The real test is whether someone can simplify complexity when it matters.
